title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs121908753 AA genotype (two copies of the CFTR R352Q variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of R352Q. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
riskSummary: CFTR variant associated with efficacy for ivacaftor

summary: TTR-related cardiac amyloidosis
detail: A person with one or two copies of the (A) allele of this SNP is at significantly higher (by about 2 - 3 fold) risk of developing cardiac amyloidosis, in which levels of the transthyretin protein build up and may lead to heart failure, especially with increasing age.

title: 20x increased risk of Ankylosing Spondylitis
summary: 20x increased risk of Ankylosing Spondylitis.
detail: Greatly increased odds (but still only about 2%) of this disease. Ankylosing Spondylitis is a rare autoimmune disease that causes inflammation of the spine and joints. The usual age of onset is from the late teens to 40 years of age. Some people with ankylosing spondylitis have onset of symptoms when younger than 16 years. This form of AS is referred to as juvenile-onset ankylosing spondylitis. Reference: http://www.emedicinehealth.com/ankylosing_spondylitis_rheumatologic_perspective/article_em.htm
